scope.files: Every repository-owned .hx file in the checkout is scanned, including compiler, target API, fixtures, examples, templates, and future runtime tooling.
scope.policy: Untyped values never enter semantic compiler logic. An unavoidable external or negative-test boundary is exact-source allowlisted, validates immediately into the named closed model, and has malformed-input evidence.

constructs.description: Haxe Any erases the static value contract.
Haxe Dynamic defers member and conversion checks.
haxe.Json.parse returns an untyped external value.
DynamicAccess is an open-ended string-keyed value carrier.
Private-access metadata bypasses the declared visibility contract.
Raw __c__ calls bypass typed IR and structural C AST lowering.
Reflect member access replaces a known structural type with runtime lookup.
The Haxe cast keyword can assert a type without validating the value shape.
The Haxe untyped keyword disables ordinary type checking.

allowlist.justification: The reviewed Reflaxe ownership file is external JSON and the pinned Haxe parser necessarily returns an untyped value.
allowlist.validation: The adapter immediately checks schema version, activity scalar types, the generated-file array, every string entry, path normalization, duplicates, and ownership-manifest exclusion before returning the closed record.

allowlist.justification: The negative aggregate fixture must present the real Haxe Dynamic semantic type to prove it cannot masquerade as a structurally known value record.
allowlist.validation: CBodyLowering rejects the signature before constructing HxcIR and the aggregate suite requires exact source-positioned HXC1001 with no generated artifact.

allowlist.justification: The negative test must forge an impossible enum-abstract value to prove the typed diagnostic registry rejects unallocated IDs.
allowlist.validation: The forged value is used only for the rejecting call and the fixture fails unless CDiagnostic.definition throws.

allowlist.justification: This fixture intentionally represents the real Haxe Dynamic language type so HxcIR can prove it is modeled explicitly rather than used as an internal escape hatch.
allowlist.validation: The HxcIR suite exercises the explicit IRTDynamic variant and validator rejection paths; the value is not part of compiler implementation state.
